reviews.review_description: I love space games and tight shooting. My one request is that without looking at my wrist I can’t tell I’m about to die. Could you make that more apparent?  Thanks team

This had a lot of potential but promised updates never came and Dev is not responding on Discord anymore. Dont buy this till the game breaking bugs are fixed, if ever. Oculus, please take this game down!

It‘s pretty straight forward what it claims to be. Fast paced entertainment when you just want to jump into some action. The graphics, the mechanic and everything is kept simple in a very good way and makes it a joy to play. Thumbstick locomotion and smooth turning in the options is all there. It feels smooth and runs very well although there are some bugs to be found. But in the worst case you could still just start another round ;) And it felt different on every new run. I didn‘t get very far in my first sessions but that probably just means I need to train my reflexes. Since it‘s in *EARLY ACCESS* right now, I‘m looking very much forward to the expected upcoming more and more polished versions!

I Love this game, play it every morning.  I hope this game keeps evolving.💥💥💥💥So now I've been playing D9 for awhile, and it is one of my #1 played games.  The developer continues to add polish, and boy does it shine.  This is my favorite genre, thank you for a solid game. Get this game its awesome.    Update- I play this game still every day.  One of my favorite games.

reviews.review_title: Really fun, but needs to be polished
reviews.review_description:
What can I say, I like Rogue-likes. And there aren't many good Rogue-like FPSes for VR and especially not so for the Oculus Quest.

Is this game fun? Yes!
But is the game polished and bug free? No, as expected from an early access game and not to mention the price difference from an officially published Oculus game which is usually 30$. 
This is just about 13$. It's totally worth it for the price. The dev hasn't put anywhere near as much original work & effort they put in for a officially published Oculus game, but last time I checked officially published dev's weren't making Rogue-like FPSes.

The AI is some what 2dish without much polish. 
AI ignores bullets. It doesn't hear or see bullets or bullet collisions with walls. Only way for an AI detect you is when it sees you or it gets hit by a bullet or when other AI in the room detects you by the previously mentioned methods. IMHO AI needs sound detection and bullet visual detection among other things.
AI doesn't even patrol. They just stay in the corners or somewhere in the room waiting for you to hit them with bullets or to see you. Patrolling with Waypoints should be added.
The above two additioned combined with some stealth mechanics would be great.

Also the dungeons feel some what 2d. There should be more upper floors or ramps etc to make the game feel less claustrophobic and like 2d top down dungeons.

Also it'd be great to enable the laser in the 3d model of the gun. The gun model has the laser and flashlight, but they don't work in game. Would be nice to have some aim assist for people who want to take it easy or are not able to aim guns properly.

There are bugs that need to be ironed out and polish to be game like AI that need to be made, but the dev seems to be fixing bugs as they are reported. I guess that's to be expected for an early access game.

TL;DR: Really fun Rogue-like FPS with cute low-poly graphics for a low price. Worth the money. But has some bugs and AI among other things needs polish.
Good shooting action and a nice atmosphere. Weapons to unlock and passive abilities to collect.
I understand that this is an app lab game, but many of the bugs make the game unplayable at the moment. I will have to get a refund for now and come back to it later

Dev is probably not dead. But the development of this game definitely was. I had joined the discord server of the dev. They were only active for maybe a month. I was hoping my suggestion of more upgrades would have been looked into. I was only mocked. 

Game actually softlocks you after a certain point. Devs do not respond to anything. It has so much potential and I wanted to love it... Oh well, I would reccomend you go play Sweet Surrender as that game is playable LMAO

Very fun game! I love roguelite games, and this one is executed well. The cycle of seeing how far you can get is very addictive. Hoping that this continues with some good added content in future updates!

Let me start by saying this game has SO much potential. BUT it's absolutely riddled by bugs. It's nearly unplayable after level 5. Your game will crash, you will fall through the floor, something will make it impossible to continue through the game. 

This would be perfectly acceptable if the developers were working on updates. The sad thing is they have completely abandoned this game, leaving unsuspecting folks to purchase this absolutely broken mess and hoping it will be fixed. The discord channel for D9 has even been abandoned by the devs. Leaving many people feeling they have been ripped off, myself included. I didn't even pay full price and I still feel ripped off. 

DO NOT BUY

Update : development has officially been canceled by the devs.
Great immersive environments and gameplay. Already dumped 3-4 hours into it.
